title: Purely Pleasant Peruvian Chicken Causa Limena
description: A garlicky and comforting Peruvian dish featuring rose water and binchotan-charcoal, with a unique glassy-jelly texture, low in fat and perfect for a weeknight dinner
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit
In a blender, combine rose water, garlic, binchotan charcoal powder, lime juice, salt, and black pepper, and blend until smooth
In a large bowl, toss the chicken breast with the blended mixture until the chicken is well coated
Place the chicken on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper and bake for 25 minutes
While the chicken is baking, boil the diced potatoes in a large pot of salted water until they are tender
Drain the potatoes and set them aside to cool
In a large skillet, heat the olive oil over medium heat and saute the diced yellow pepper until it is tender
Once the chicken is done, let it rest for 5 minutes before slicing it into thin strips
To assemble the causa limena, place a layer of cooled potatoes on a serving plate, followed by a layer of sliced chicken, and then a layer of sauteed yellow pepper
Repeat the layers one more time, ending with a layer of potatoes on top
